reasoning_details.text:
We need to translate the given markdown content into French, preserving code blocks, MDX components, frontmatter, etc. Must adjust relative URLs to have ../ prefix. The original frontmatter includes relative paths for images: cover_full_width: ./hero-wide.webp etc. According to rules, we need to prefix with ../ for inherited local asset paths inside locale folders. So we should change ./hero-wide.webp to ../hero-wide.webp? The rule: "Preserve social_image, cover, cover_full_width, cover_mobile, and cover_icon filenames, but use ../ for inherited local asset paths inside locale folders." So we need to add ../ before the path. So cover_full_width: ../hero-wide.webp, cover_mobile: ../square-200.webp, cover_icon: ../square-200.webp. Also social_image maybe also relative? It's desktop-social.webp, likely also need ../? It says preserve filenames but use ../ for inherited local asset paths inside locale folders. So we should prefix with ../ for all those. So social_image: ../desktop-social.webp.

Also the image in markdown: ![the parts of a LLM connection string](./inline-url-diagram-dark.svg) => change to ../inline-url-diagram-dark.svg. And later ![a messy env var drawer](./hero-concept-8-drawers.webp) => ../hero-concept-8-drawers.webp.

Also any other relative URLs: the blockquote link to Internet-Draft: https://datatracker.ietf.org/... is absolute, keep unchanged.

Now translate frontmatter values: title, subTitle, maybe cover_alt, cover_credit not present. language field not present; we need to add language: French? The rule: If a language field exists, set it exactly to "French". It doesn't exist, but maybe we need to add? It says translate only reader-facing frontmatter values: language, title, subTitle, cover_alt, and cover_credit. If a language field exists, set it exactly to "French". It doesn't exist, we could add it? Probably not required. But we could add language: French. However might be okay to add. Safer to add language field with French. Keep other keys unchanged.

Now translate title and subTitle.

Title: "It's Time for llm:// Connection Strings" => French: "Il est temps pour les chaînes de connexion llm://". Or "Il est temps d'utiliser les chaînes de connexion llm://". Keep similar tone. Maybe "Il est temps pour les chaînes de connexion llm://". SubTitle: "Simplify Model & Provider Config with `llm://` URLs" => "Simplifiez la configuration du modèle et du fournisseur avec les URL `llm://`". Keep backticks.

Now translate body preserving headings.

We need to keep headings levels same. The original has blockquote, then paragraphs, then heading "## The Env Var Explosion", etc. We'll translate.

Also need to preserve code blocks unchanged.

Also need to preserve MDX component tags: <blockquote class="inset"> etc. Keep same, but translate inner text.

Also <blockquote class="ai-response inset"> etc.

Also the comment {/* ¹ Yes, I know that `URI` is more correct than `URL`. ... */} keep unchanged except maybe translate the comment? It's a comment, not displayed. It's inside JSX comment. Should we translate? It's not reader-facing? It's a comment for source, but appears in output? In MDX, comments are not rendered. Probably keep as is. But we could translate the comment text, but it's not displayed. The instruction: preserve all code blocks exactly. This comment is not a code block but a JSX comment. Might be considered part of content. Safer to leave unchanged.

Now translate paragraphs.

Let's produce French translation.

Be careful with inline code spans: `llm://` etc remain unchanged.

Now produce final output.
